params.text:
Your personal development

Care Certificate Standard 2 – Your personal development is about how you keep learning and improving throughout your career. Good care depends on workers who reflect on their practice and continue to build their knowledge and skills.

Key parts of your development include:

  • A Personal Development Plan (PDP) — a written plan, usually reviewed each year, setting out what you need to learn, how you will learn it, and how progress will be checked
  • Supervision and appraisal — regular one-to-one meetings with your manager to discuss your work, your wellbeing, feedback and your goals
  • Feedback and reflection — using comments from managers, colleagues and the people you support to recognise strengths and identify areas to improve
  • Ongoing learning through training, e-learning, shadowing and mentoring

Completing the Care Certificate is often one of your first development milestones, showing you can work safely and confidently in your role.

params.question: Supervision meetings are only used to point out a care worker's mistakes.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Not quite. Supervision is a supportive, two-way discussion covering wellbeing, goals, feedback and learning, not only errors.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct. Supervision is a two-way conversation about your wellbeing, your goals, feedback and development — not simply about mistakes.
